cards.front: What is the primary goal when taking 10 different random samples?
cards.back: To estimate population parameters and improve the accuracy of statistical models

cards.front: What is the purpose of taking multiple random samples?
cards.back: To reduce sample bias and increase the reliability of statistical inferences

cards.front: How many samples is typically considered sufficient for reliable estimates?
cards.back: 10 to 20 samples, depending on the sample size and desired level of precision

cards.front: What happens when taking multiple random samples with the same characteristics?
cards.back: The estimates and inferences become more accurate and reliable due to the reduction of sample bias

cards.front: What is the benefit of taking multiple random samples in machine learning models?
cards.back: Improved model generalizability and reduced overfitting to individual samples
